input: Python Methods and Lists: Methods perform actions and require parentheses: object.method(). Attributes store information without parentheses: object.attribute. String methods: str.lower() returns lowercase copy. List methods: list.sort() sorts ascending in-place, list.sort(reverse=True) descending, list.append(item) adds to end. .sort() modifies original list and returns None. Use reverse=True to switch sort order. Key distinction: methods (actions) vs attributes (stored info).
